I think that's a big thing. They wanted to buy a computer anyway, they couldn't do it, and it was an excuse to buy a computer. I think that had a lot to do with it. Even if we don't get the business, at least we'll have a computer, John. So yeah, I think that had a lot to do with it. Try to bring into your program anything and everything that will work. I wanted to mention one other thing I told Dan I wanted to tell you. I've been to a hundred meetings like this. My wife used to tell me, we need a new couch. What are you doing to go on to a new meeting? And I said it, she said, what do you get out of these meetings? And I said, well, if I can just get one little thing, a phrase, a sentence, an idea, something I can put over here that I can move this to here and all this over here and this over here. And that's what I've watched you. That's what y'all are doing. Did I answer your question? Yes, you did. Thank you very much. All right. Big guy, Bill, you want to go next? George, as you look back over your career, any regrets, anything that you would want to do over on, any big lessons, something that didn't go well or that you would have done differently as you look back at it? No, I think everything I did was wonderful. I'm not bullshitting you. My wife thinks it's awful. You just can't be that positive. What aren't you telling me, she says? I don't know. I can't answer you. I think I did everything wonderful. That's what I intended to do. Now, without giving you details and sucking up a bunch of time, there were some very ugly episodes of this career. But in reality, the overall outcome, they didn't matter. I had my top sales manager in office one time. And I said to him, and I was sincere, I said to him, I don't understand. We have about 200 people in the office at that time. If you want to ever know what the problems of the world are, have 200 people in office, you find them out real quick. And I said, I don't understand how these people have all these problems. I said, I don't have any of them. I don't have the problems. Larry says, George, you have more damn problems than anybody I ever knew of. You're too stupid to know about. And I said, Larry, that may be true, but it's working. All right, big guy, you were next. George, I want to know what type of commission did you pay the sales guys? They all made between 100,000 and 200,000 a year. I know that. What amount? I just I don't remember. Yeah, just percentage wise. I can't remember what it was. One of the most brilliant things I ever did, as he said, modestly, was I decided that I couldn't run my office real well, that my office people could run it better than I could if I would give them a chance. So I put them all on a bonus. I took a percentage of the income every year. And we had a Christmas meeting where they all got bonuses usually from 10 to 20,000 dollars each. And they got bonuses percentage of the income. Now, what that did for me is I never fired anybody because the people got them working. And I got to tell you, I only fired probably seven people in my whole life. Because I would transfer them. I would say, no, wait a minute, we have 12 departments in this company. We have 12 managers. If you can't work, work for one of those managers, I don't think you can work for anybody. Now, which one do you think you can work for? And what type of do you want? Because I want you to be successful with us. I also hired people that nobody else wanted. A lot of those people that were in my office couldn't possibly get a job any place else. But they did wonderful things for me. Because I loved them and they loved me and we told each other every day and we really had a good time and I ain't bullshit. It worked. It was fun. We could back the percentage out probably if we did some calculations of how many units an average guy did in a year in order to. But it's less important than the fact that I just don't know what percent. It's less important than. I don't have to worry about income. I want to make over 100. That's exactly right. It's less important than that number, right? Is what the income needs to be for the person, for the caliber of person you want. To be in that position. Well, then you've got to structure it backwards so that they can reasonably hit or exceed that number. One last quick question, Dan. Before one time you spoke about Georgia's leads and you said you went to the office and they were all under rocks. Bricks. Bricks to be technical. This one guy had piles of them under bricks. Yeah, he had the incoming call slips sorted by who had called in three times, who had called in twice because they had so many leads coming in at one point. Like you said, they're hiring three salespeople this month, three more. I mean, literally these leads were I mean, this thing was. Oh, that's what I told you I want to talk about. Did you just rudely interrupt me? No, I plightly interrupt you. Very good. I spent a lot of money on advertising. I can remember spending more than my profits on advertising when I first started. I saw it. I saw the sales. I knew what was going to happen and I knew where I was going. And I think the biggest thing I can give you today is my life has been planned from the day I started. May I go into this? If you must. Yeah, go ahead. I'm unable to pick it up and I'm not criticizing because they do wonderful at other things. But the ability I have to motivate people and the biggest thing I can do in my life is to get other people to do things.
